name: Plane Ready to GitHub Issue
purpose: Create one GitHub issue when a Plane task is moved to Ready, then write the GitHub link back to Plane.

security.recommended: Use a Plane webhook secret header and reject requests where the header is missing or incorrect.

nodes.name: Receive Plane task update
nodes.notes: Receives Plane issue update events from the cloud Plane webhook.

nodes.name: CONFIG
nodes.notes: First workflow node after the trigger. Store all shared constants here instead of n8n global variables.

values.slack_queue_channel: Optional Slack channel for queued work

nodes.name: Verify webhook secret
nodes.condition: Header secret matches n8n credential value.

outputs.plane_issue_key: Human readable issue key if available
outputs.existing_github_issue_url: GitHub issue URL custom property or parsed from comments

nodes.name: No existing GitHub issue link
nodes.on_false: Respond 200 with existing GitHub issue URL.

nodes.name: Search existing GitHub issue
nodes.on_error: Notify Slack if configured, add Plane comment if possible, respond 502.

nodes.name: Create GitHub issue
nodes.on_error: Notify Slack if configured, add Plane comment if possible, respond 502.

nodes.name: Update Plane with GitHub issue link
nodes.request: Use Plane API to add a comment and/or set a GitHub issue custom field.
nodes.on_error: Notify Slack; do not retry GitHub issue creation.

safe_failure_handling: Never create a second GitHub issue if a Plane issue already has a GitHub URL.
Search GitHub by Plane issue ID before creating a new issue.
On GitHub API failure, leave Plane in Ready and add a failure comment when possible.
On Plane update failure after GitHub issue creation, notify Slack and return the GitHub issue URL for manual repair.
Do not include API tokens or webhook secrets in Slack or Plane comments.
